core_payload.content:
# Background Agent Runtime Control Plane

## 1. Boundary
Background Agent Runtime Control Plane owns the continuity and operator-legibility layer of long-lived agent work: assignment state, memory state, instruction loading, context shaping, harness semantics, subagent composition, evaluation traces, and runtime control surfaces. This sub-hub turns a daemon process into a governed runtime instead of a loose wrapper around prompt text.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups the atoms that preserve identity and explainability across runs: `agent-async-assignment`, `agent-control-plane`, `agent-memory-state`, `agent-context-engineering`, `agent-harness-protocol`, `agent-instruction-surfaces`, `agent-subagents`, `agent-evals-and-traces`, and `ai-control-surface`. Together they define how work is addressed, resumed, observed, constrained, and explained across surfaces.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own daemon bring-up, model-cost posture, workspace safety gates, or queue/job execution policy. Those remain in `daemon-lifecycle`, `mutation-gates`, and `work-orchestration` so the control plane can stay focused on continuity, accountability, and operator visibility.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Background Agent Runtime Control Plane is the continuity layer of the runtime. It groups assignment handles, memory state, instruction loading, context engineering, harness semantics, subagent composition, evaluation traces, and operator control surfaces so long-lived work stays legible across runs. This boundary matters because a daemon becomes governable only when assignment continuity, memory continuity, and visible control stay explicit instead of dissolving into host lifecycle or job queues. It gives the runtime one clear seam for accountability, operator visibility, and session continuity while leaving policy gates and execution work elsewhere.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of background-agent-runtime hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 44 outbound to ≤15.

Expanded the control-plane summary to satisfy validator length requirements and normalized the semantic hash to an eight-token canonical form without altering assignment or memory continuity doctrine.
